hyperedges.rationale: Together these concepts form the ask/tell lifecycle contract for asynchronous optimizer evaluation.
These identities separate schema, optimizer configuration, and decoded search-point identity for reproducibility and safe comparison.
Checkpoint resume uses the stable envelope and optimizer payload while preserving event history as audit continuity rather than replayable state.

nodes.label: Rust-native Python optimization
nodes.rationale: Python owns the ergonomic public API while Rust owns hot paths through evocore._core.

nodes.label: IntegerMarginDistribution
nodes.rationale: Protects integer probability mass so integer genes do not collapse too quickly.

nodes.label: Custom operators
nodes.rationale: Custom operators are structured objects so compatibility and reproducibility metadata can be recorded instead of accepting opaque bare callables.

nodes.label: Optimizer config reproducibility
nodes.rationale: Canonical optimizer configuration is hashed separately from runtime hooks so deterministic metadata remains trustworthy.

nodes.label: Checkpoint telemetry continuity
nodes.rationale: Telemetry can be carried for audit continuity, but checkpoint state remains the source of optimizer resume truth.

nodes.label: Evaluator-owned parallelism
nodes.rationale: Expensive evaluation strategy belongs inside the Evaluator, allowing pools, remote jobs, caches, and rate limits to be hidden behind evaluate().

nodes.label: Objective and evaluation semantics
nodes.rationale: Objective semantics are the next layer after GeneSpace because optimizers need stable interpretation and accounting of observations.

nodes.label: Budget and termination contract
nodes.rationale: Shared stop and budget semantics should be defined before island models or broader orchestration.

nodes.label: Optimizer configuration contract
nodes.rationale: Each optimizer needs exportable comparable public config so reproducibility metadata can be trusted.

nodes.label: Stateless operator RNG
nodes.rationale: Operators derive stack-local StdRng instances from seed inputs so no RNG state escapes operator calls.

nodes.label: CMA-ES mirror folding
nodes.rationale: Mirror-folding preserves sampling distribution shape near boundaries better than clipping.

nodes.label: CMA-ES two-array loop
nodes.rationale: Continuous samples update CMA state while rounded or clamped samples are passed to the user's fitness function.

nodes.label: Full-genome fixed numeric genes
nodes.rationale: Fixed genes stay in GeneSpace, Individual.genes, and Individual.params to keep the public schema full-genome.

nodes.label: Python Package Domain Restructure Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: python package domain restructure implementation plan

nodes.label: CMA-ES Rust State Snapshots Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: cma-es rust state snapshots implementation plan

nodes.label: Checkpoint Compatibility Golden Fixtures Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: checkpoint compatibility golden fixtures implementation plan

nodes.label: GA Mixed Bool Support Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: ga mixed bool support implementation plan

nodes.label: Differential Evolution Feature Parity Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: differential evolution feature parity implementation plan

nodes.label: DE Rust Kernel Migration Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: de rust kernel migration implementation plan

nodes.label: DE Rust Kernel Adapter Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: de rust kernel adapter implementation plan

nodes.label: DE Strategy Metadata Contract Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: de strategy metadata contract implementation plan

nodes.label: Optimizer Lifecycle Helper Consolidation Implementation Plan
nodes.norm_label: optimizer lifecycle helper consolidation implementation plan

nodes.label: Objective Evaluation Semantics Contract
nodes.rationale: Valid state-updating records require finite raw scores, rejected records use score=None, and callable paths should raise instead of silently sanitizing non-finite values.

nodes.label: Cached Observations Budget Semantics
nodes.rationale: Cached observations may update optimizer state, but only fresh trusted_full records spend full-evaluation budget.

nodes.label: Candidate/Solution Boundary
nodes.rationale: Candidates retain lifecycle identity and evaluator ergonomics, while Solutions expose result-facing values and provenance without carrying scheduler state.

nodes.label: Checkpointed Eigen Cache State
nodes.rationale: The lazy eigendecomposition cache is treated as algorithm state because discarding it can change the next ask sequence after restore.

nodes.label: Python-Owned Optimizer Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: The Rust kernel owns deterministic DE proposal math, while Python continues to own candidates, replacement, jDE commit/discard, checkpoints, events, telemetry, policies, callbacks, and evaluator integration.

nodes.label: Rust Gene Codec Parity Implementation Plan
nodes.summary: Plan to consolidate Rust-internal gene kind parsing and encoded repair for DE and reproduction kernels while preserving PyO3 signatures.
nodes.norm_label: rust gene codec parity implementation plan

nodes.label: src/gene_codec.rs
nodes.summary: New Rust internal module intended to own parse_gene_kind, parse_gene_kinds, repair_encoded_value, and repair_encoded_values.

nodes.label: Encoded Repair Contract
nodes.summary: Shared behavior clamps float genes, rounds and clamps int genes, and thresholds bool genes at 0.5.

nodes.label: Search-Space Codec Contract Implementation Plan
nodes.summary: Plan to make Python search-space encode, decode, and repair helpers canonical across operators, DE, and CMA-ES.

nodes.label: Search-space codec helpers
nodes.summary: decode_gene_values, encode_gene_values, repair_gene_value, and repair_gene_values centralize Python decoded-value repair and Rust-boundary encoding.

nodes.label: OperatorCodec compatibility facade
nodes.summary: OperatorCodec remains importable while delegating encode/decode behavior to shared search-space helpers.

nodes.label: evocore v1 Design Spec
nodes.summary: Initial Rust-native Python library design for GA and CMA-ES, targeting DEAP speedups through a Python API layer and PyO3/Rust hot paths.

nodes.label: Two-layer Rust/Python architecture
nodes.summary: Python owns API ergonomics while evocore._core and Rust own hot-path operators, CMA-ES, selection, and parallelism.

nodes.label: evocore v2 Design Spec
nodes.summary: Draft design extending v1 with GeneSpace, integer genes, fitness caching, deterministic Rust RNG, process/thread parallelism, checkpointing, and multi-run support.

nodes.label: GeneSpace
nodes.summary: Central per-gene type, bounds, and naming concept introduced to replace flat gene_bounds plus gene_length configuration.

nodes.label: evocore v3 Design Spec
nodes.summary: Design replacing mutable RNG state with hierarchical derive_seed and consolidating GA reproduction into a single Rust call.

nodes.label: Hierarchical seed derivation
nodes.summary: All random decisions derive from master_seed, generation, individual index, and operation type to guarantee thread-count-independent reproducibility.

nodes.label: evocore v4 Design Spec
nodes.summary: Design resolving v3 issues around spawn-based process parallelism, PyO3 f64 boundary encoding, CMA-ES continuous samples, GenerationInfo callbacks, and opt-in diversity history.

nodes.label: GenerationInfo
nodes.summary: Structured callback context carrying generation, nan_fitness_count, and cached_count, replacing a dedicated on_fitness_warning hook.

nodes.label: evocore v5 Design Spec
nodes.summary: Design fixing v4 follow-ups: _run_child_engine importability, ProcessPoolExecutor teardown, CMAESEngine process parallel guard, and run_multiple wall-clock accounting.

nodes.label: Process parallel teardown
nodes.summary: ProcessParallel and run_multiple use try/finally and shutdown(cancel_futures=True, wait=False) to avoid worker hangs after exceptions.

nodes.label: Evocore v0.6.0 Production Hardening Design
nodes.summary: Production hardening release design covering CI, quality gates, typed packaging, docs, cross-platform wheels, PyPI publication, runtime logging/version polish, and property tests.

nodes.label: Typed packaging
nodes.summary: py.typed and _core.pyi become part of the wheel contract so downstream users and type checkers can rely on package types.

nodes.label: GA Fixed Genes And Budget Design
nodes.summary: Design adding fixed numeric genes as full-genome members plus exact GA evaluation-budget control and run diagnostics.
nodes.norm_label: ga fixed genes and budget design

nodes.label: Fixed numeric genes
nodes.summary: GeneDef accepts equal numeric bounds so a fixed value remains part of Individual.genes, params, callbacks, logbooks, and dataframes while operators preserve it.

nodes.label: EvoCore vNext Expensive Optimizer Design
nodes.summary: Architecture pivot from DEAP parity toward ask/tell-native, multi-fidelity, racing-aware, surrogate-assisted, mixed-variable expensive black-box optimization.

nodes.label: Expensive black-box optimization
nodes.summary: Product direction where evaluation budget is managed as a scarce optimization resource instead of evaluating every candidate at full cost.

nodes.label: Successive Halving
nodes.summary: Multi-fidelity resource allocation reference used to justify adaptive promotion of promising configurations.

nodes.label: Hyperband
nodes.summary: Resource allocation reference cited for evaluating many configurations cheaply and promoting survivors.

nodes.label: CatCMA and CatCMAwM
nodes.summary: Mixed-variable CMA references motivating categorical distributions and integer/categorical support beyond naive rounding.

nodes.label: Financial anti-overfit telemetry
nodes.summary: Trial accounting and lineage needed by White's Reality Check, Deflated Sharpe Ratio, Hansen SPA, and Model Confidence Set workflows.

nodes.label: Explicit Batch Tokens Design
nodes.summary: Design adding deterministic public batch_id values to candidates and optional batch_id on evaluation records for asynchronous partial tell semantics.

nodes.label: Batch ledger
nodes.summary: Internal ledger keyed by batch_id storing candidate order, records, consumed state, and engine-specific payloads.

nodes.label: Optimizer Lifecycle Protocols Design
nodes.summary: Clean-break public ask/tell lifecycle contract for single-objective optimizers, evaluators, candidates, evaluation records, contexts, tell results, and state summaries.

nodes.label: Optimizer protocol
nodes.summary: Structural protocol with direction, ask, tell, and state_summary methods implemented by GAEngine and CMAESEngine.

nodes.label: EvaluationRecord
nodes.summary: Only public observation accepted by tell, carrying candidate_id, score, confidence, rung, and optional batch/cost/metrics/metadata.

nodes.label: GeneSpace Contract Design
nodes.summary: Design making GeneSpace the canonical owner of flat search-space identity, validation, signatures, JSON export, and reproducibility hashing.

nodes.label: Canonical GeneSpace signature
nodes.summary: JSON-safe payload including schema_version, ordered genes, has_names, length, and per-gene fixed metadata.

nodes.label: Objective And Evaluation Semantics Design
nodes.summary: Design defining strict raw score, direction, confidence, failure, cache, repeated observation, state, best, and budget semantics.

nodes.label: Evaluation confidence semantics
nodes.summary: trusted_full and cached are state/best eligible; only trusted_full consumes fresh full budget; partial, surrogate, and rejected remain history-visible but not state-eligible.

nodes.label: Result History Telemetry Contract Design
nodes.summary: Design stabilizing OptimizationResult, GenerationHistory, EventHistory, OptimizationTelemetry, deterministic exports, pandas exports, and reproducibility metadata.

nodes.label: EventHistory
nodes.summary: Append-only event observations for ask, tell, and generation events, forming an audit surface separate from compact generation summaries.

nodes.label: Budget And Termination Contract Design
nodes.summary: Design defining shared max_evaluations, max_generations, n_evaluations, stop_reason vocabulary, result diagnostics, and public API cleanup.

nodes.label: Stop reason vocabulary
nodes.summary: Shared reasons include max_evaluations, max_generations, callback, manual, optimizer_converged, and reserved future target_score, patience, and wall_time names.

nodes.label: Operator Contract Design
nodes.summary: Design making GA variation, selection, bounds behavior, typed operators, compatibility validation, mutation sigma semantics, and custom operator protocols a public contract.

nodes.label: BoundsPolicy.clamp
nodes.summary: Named public repair policy: clamp floats, round and clamp ints, threshold bools, and preserve fixed numeric genes.

nodes.label: Optimizer Configuration Contract Design
nodes.summary: Design for exportable, comparable, JSON-safe optimizer configuration signatures, hashes, compatibility validation, and hook-aware reproducibility metadata.

nodes.label: Optimizer config hash
nodes.summary: Stable SHA-256 hash over a canonical optimizer configuration signature, separate from the GeneSpace hash.

nodes.label: Python Package Domain Restructure Design
nodes.summary: Breaking public API migration from flat modules to domain-oriented packages with score-oriented vocabulary and focused implementation modules.

nodes.label: Domain-oriented package architecture
nodes.summary: Target layout organizes EvoCore into core, search_space, lifecycle, results, optimizers, callbacks, and surrogates packages.

nodes.label: Ask/Tell Checkpoints Design
nodes.summary: Design extending stable checkpoint envelopes to GA ask/tell runtime ledgers so pending batches, partial tells, telemetry, and event history can resume.

nodes.label: GA ask/tell checkpoint payload
nodes.summary: Checkpoint state_kind ga_ask_tell stores event_index, candidates, batches, trusted candidate IDs, best candidate ID, telemetry, and event rows.

nodes.label: Candidate/Solution Boundary Design
nodes.summary: Design making Candidate lifecycle records and Solution population/result records explicit, with conversion helpers and GeneSpace-owned value hashing.

nodes.label: GeneSpace value hash
nodes.summary: Schema-aware search-point identity that validates values, encodes by declared kind, and includes gene_space_hash to avoid cross-schema collisions.

nodes.label: Checkpoint Resume Contract Design
nodes.summary: Long-term checkpoint/resume contract separating result exports, event audit data, and authoritative optimizer continuation snapshots, with GA supported first and CMA deferred.

nodes.label: Stable checkpoint envelope
nodes.summary: JSON-safe checkpoint schema with optimizer identity, position, state payload, audit data, and metadata for validated continuation.

links.rationale: The plan explicitly creates src/gene_codec.rs as the shared internal module.
The module owns repair_encoded_value and repair_encoded_values.
Both plans centralize float/int/bool repair semantics at different language boundaries.
The plan adds module-level encode, decode, and repair helpers.
OperatorCodec methods are specified to delegate to shared helpers.
Both define public repair behavior for floats, ints, bools, and fixed genes.
The v1 architecture section defines Rust for speed and Python for API ergonomics.
The v2 spec states that it supersedes the 2026-04-04 design.
GeneSpace is listed as a key v2 improvement and has its own API section.
The v3 spec states that it supersedes the v2 design.
Fixed numeric genes are expressed as GeneDef/GeneSpace metadata.
The v3 core change replaces RNG state with derive_seed.
The v4 spec states that it supersedes the v3 design.
v4 adds GenerationInfo and updates callback signatures.
The v5 spec states that it supersedes the v4 design.
v5 specifies try/finally teardown for ProcessParallel and run_multiple.
The production hardening design lists typed packaging as a release goal.
The design allows equal numeric bounds and requires operators to preserve fixed values.
The design cites CatCMA and CatCMAwM for mixed-variable CMA behavior.
The vNext design states the capability gap is no longer speed alone but managing full-cost fitness calls.
The design lists anti-overfit telemetry requirements for downstream financial gates.
The research grounding section links Hyperband.
The research grounding section links Successive Halving.
The batch-token design defines a private batch ledger keyed by batch_id.
Both designs make batch_id part of evaluation-record validation.
The lifecycle design defines EvaluationRecord as the only accepted tell observation.
The protocol design adds Optimizer and Evaluator structural protocols.
The target layout places shared ask/tell contracts under evocore.lifecycle.
The GeneSpace contract defines signature, hash, to_dict, to_json, and validation.
Gene-space signatures and optimizer config hashes are separate reproducibility identities used together.
Value hashes include gene_space_hash and validate through GeneSpace.
The objective semantics design defines confidence categories and their state, best, and budget behavior.
The budget design explicitly aligns max_evaluations with fresh trusted_full records from the objective semantics.
The result contract adds EventRecord and EventHistory as append-only audit data.
The checkpoint contract states that EventHistory is audit data and not replay input.
The budget contract defines stop_reason as the authoritative final status vocabulary.
The operator contract names clamp as the v1 bounds policy.
The configuration contract includes optimizer components such as crossover, mutation, schedule, selection, and future repair/replacement.
The config contract exposes config_signature and config_hash as public methods.
The checkpoint envelope validates optimizer_config_hash before resume.
The restructure design defines the target domain package tree.
The ask/tell checkpoint design defines the GA state payload.
Ask/tell checkpoints explicitly reuse the shared stable CheckpointSnapshot envelope.
The boundary design adds GeneSpace.value_signature and value_hash.
The checkpoint contract recommends a shared JSON-safe checkpoint envelope.

hyperedges.rationale: Together these concepts form the ask/tell lifecycle contract for asynchronous optimizer evaluation.
These identities separate schema, optimizer configuration, and decoded search-point identity for reproducibility and safe comparison.
Checkpoint resume uses the stable envelope and optimizer payload while preserving event history as audit continuity rather than replayable state.
